initial.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

```asp
% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall color (the background) is 1, new interior color is 2
wall(1).
new_color(2).

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells (any cell on the outermost row or column)
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0; C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = N-1; C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0; R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = M-1; R = _.

% Flood‑fill from the boundary using only cells that are not of the wall colour
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
            input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% Each cell keeps its original colour (the default case)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,Color).

% Cells that are of the wall colour and are not reachable from the boundary
% become the new interior colour.
output(R,C,new_color(NC)) :-
    output(R,C,Color0),
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% Ensure that no other cells are modified
:- not (output(R,C,Color), Color != 0, Color != 1, Color != 2, Color != 3, Color != 4, Color != 5, Color != 6, Color != 7, Color != 8, Color != 9).

% Helper to map the cell to its output position (here the grid size is unchanged,
% so the cell is the same)
output_cell(R,C) :- true.

% Prevent the program from producing alternative answers by fixing the above
% constraints.
:- 1.
```

```
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Boundary definition (any cell on the outer edge)

boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(X), C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(X), C = _.

outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
            input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% Default: each cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,Color).

% Interior cells that are of colour 1 and are not outside become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% Ensure that no other cell is changed – we let ASP choose the unique answer set.
% (no extra constraints needed – the above rules already determine a unique solution)
```

```
% Determine dimensions
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Border helpers
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R : input(R, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Adjacent offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0; C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_), C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_), C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0; R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_), R = _.

% Flood‑fill from the boundary through cells that are not of colour 1 (the wall)
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
            input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% Default – keep the original colour of each cell
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(R,C,Color).

% Interior colour‑1 cells become colour‑2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% End of program – force a single answer set
:- 1.
```

```asp
% Determine dimensions (optional)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Wall colour (the colour that acts as a barrier) is 1 and the interior colour is 2
wall(1).
new_colour(2).

% 4‑connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Cells on the outer edge of the grid
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0; C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}; C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}; C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0; R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}; R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}; R = _.

% Flood‑fill from the boundary using only cells whose colour is NOT the wall colour
outside(R,C) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
            input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% Default: keep the original colour of each cell
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% Cells that have the wall colour (1) and are NOT reachable from the boundary become the interior colour (2)
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% End of program – enforce a unique answer set
:- 1.
```

full_program:
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the input grid (rows and columns are 0‑based)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let max_row = NRows - 1.
let max_col = NCols - 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall colour – colour at the top‑left corner (0,0)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
wall(Wall) :- input(0, 0, Wall).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells on the outer edge that are NOT the wall colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col;  input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                   R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                   input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – not reachable and not on the boundary
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- not reachable(R,C), not boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells that are NOT the wall and NOT zero.
%  These are the candidates for the colour that will be copied into
%  interior walls.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior_nonwall(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                          V != Wall, V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  The colour of the first interior non‑wall cell (row‑major order)
%  – this colour will be used for all interior walls.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
intColour(Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R,C,Colour),
    not exists_earlier(R,C,Colour).

exists_earlier(R,C,Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R1,C1,V1),
    V1 != Wall,
    V1 != 0,
    (R1 < R);
    or (R1 = R, C1 < C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definitions
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Boundary walls stay wall colour
output(R,C,Wall) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).

%  2.  Reachable non‑wall cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

%  3.  Interior zero cells become 4 (as in the reference example)
output(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).

%  4.  Interior non‑zero, non‑wall cells keep their own colour
output(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                V != 0, V != Wall,
                not interior_wall(R,C).

%  5.  Interior walls copy the colour chosen for interior walls
interior_wall(R,C) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,Colour) :- interior_wall(R,C), intColour(Colour), Colour != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every output cell must have exactly one colour and the whole
%  (NRows × NCols) region must be covered.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
#count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = (max_row + 1) * (max_col + 1).
:- 1.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>

min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Input size (optional – kept for completeness)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 2.  Helper predicates for the outer edge of the grid
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% 3.  4‑connected neighbours (up, down, left, right)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% 4.  Boundary cells – any cell on the first/last row or column
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_); R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = min_col(_); R = _.

% 5.  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour (wall = 1)
outside(R,C)  :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% 6.  Default – every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% 7.  Interior colour‑1 cells (colour 1 is the “wall”) become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% 8.  Force a single answer set – the rules above determine a unique
%    interpretation of the transformation.
:- 1.
</asp>

% Determine dimensions:
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R: input(R,_,_)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C: input(_,C,_)}.

% Output dimensions:
output_rows(P) :- P = N*N.
output_cols(Q) :- Q = M*M.

% Source placement:
output_cell(R,C,Color) :-
    input(BR,BC,V), V != 0,
    input(DR,DC,_),
    N = NRows, M = NCols,
    R = BR*N + DR, C = BC*M + DC,
    Color = V.

% For zero cells:
output_cell(R,C,TileColor) :-
    input(BR,BC,0),
    input(DR,DC,TileColor),
    N = NRows, M = NCols,
    R = BR*N + DR, C = BC*M + DC.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(NRows) :- NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NCols) :- NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Tile the input.
%  * Non‑zero cells copy the whole input – they overwrite any later
%    (lower‑priority) copies.
%  * Zero cells copy the whole input but are only applied where no
%    later copy (i.e. a non‑zero copy) has already written the cell.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

%  Non‑zero source copies (high priority)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(SR,SC,V),      V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

%  Zero source copies (low priority)
output(R,C,Tile) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,Tile),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).               % no higher‑priority copy writes here

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce exactly one colour per output cell and that the whole
%  output region (NRows × NRows by NCols × NCols) is covered.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C : output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<attempt_1>
<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Input size (optional – kept for completeness)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 2.  Helper predicates for the outer edge of the grid
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% 3.  4‑connected neighbours (up, down, left, right)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% 4.  Boundary cells – any cell on the first/last row or column
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_); R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = min_col(_); R = _.

% 5.  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour (wall = 1)
outside(R,C)  :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% 6.  Default – every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% 7.  Interior colour‑1 cells (colour 1 is the “wall”) become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% 8.  Force a single answer set – the rules above determine a unique
%    interpretation of the transformation.
:- 1.
</asp>

<attempt_2>
<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(NRows) :- NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NCols) :- NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Tile the input.
%  * Non‑zero cells copy the whole input – they overwrite any later
%    (lower‑priority) copies.
%  * Zero cells copy the whole input but are only applied where no
%    later copy (i.e. a non‑zero copy) has already written the cell.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

%  Non‑zero source copies (high priority)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(SR,SC,V),      V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

%  Zero source copies (low priority)
output(R,C,Tile) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,Tile),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).               % no higher‑priority copy writes here

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce exactly one colour per output cell and that the whole
%  output region (NRows × NRows by NCols × NCols) is covered.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C : output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

```
let max_row = #max{R : input(R,_,_)}.
let max_col = #max{C : input(_,C,_)}.
let NRows = max_row + 1.
let NCols = max_col + 1.

% High priority copy from non-zero input cell
output(R,C,V) :-
    input(SR,SC,V), V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

% Low priority zero copy
output(R,C,0) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).   % ensure not overwritten by higher priority copy

% Force exactly one color per output cell and that all cells are covered
:- #count{R,C:output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
```

```
let max_row = #max{R:input(R,_,_)}.
```

```
</asp>
```

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<attempt_1>
<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Input size (optional – kept for completeness)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 2.  Helper predicates for the outer edge of the grid
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% 3.  4‑connected neighbours (up, down, left, right)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% 4.  Boundary cells – any cell on the first/last row or column
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_); R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = min_col(_); R = _.

% 5.  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour (wall = 1)
outside(R,C)  :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% 6.  Default – every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% 7.  Interior colour‑1 cells (colour 1 is the “wall”) become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% 8.  Force a single answer set – the rules above determine a unique
%    interpretation of the transformation.
:- 1.
</asp>

<attempt_2>
<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(NRows) :- NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NCols) :- NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Tile the input.
%  * Non‑zero cells copy the whole input – they overwrite any later
%    (lower‑priority) copies.
%  * Zero cells copy the whole input but are only applied where no
%    later copy (i.e. a non‑zero copy) has already written the cell.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

%  Non‑zero source copies (high priority)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(SR,SC,V),      V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

%  Zero source copies (low priority)
output(R,C,Tile) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,Tile),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).               % no higher‑priority copy writes here

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce exactly one colour per output cell and that the whole
%  output region (NRows × NRows by NCols × NCols) is covered.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C : output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
let max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let NRows = max_row + 1.
let NCols = max_col + 1.

% copy the whole input for a non‑zero source cell
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(SR, SC, Vsrc), Vsrc != 0,
    input(TR, TC, V),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC.

% zero block for a zero source cell
output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(SR, SC, 0),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R, C, _).

% exactly one colour for every output cell and the whole output region is covered
:- #count{R, C : output(R, C, _) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% For each possible output cell (R,C), determine the color that finally appears.
% Output is the highest colour among copies that reach the cell.
output(R, C, Color) :-
    hasCopy(R, C, Color),
    not existsHigher(R, C, Color).

% There exists a copy (source cell + offset) that writes the colour Color at (R,C)
hasCopy(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR, SC, Color),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = #eval(SR * NRows + TR),
    C = #eval(SC * NCols + TC).

% There exists a copy with a strictly higher colour that also reaches the cell.
existsHigher(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR2, SC2, Value2),
    Value2 != 0,
    Value2 > Color,
    input(TR2, TC2, _),
    R = #eval(SR2 * NRows + TR2),
    C = #eval(SC2 * NCols + TC2).

% Every output cell must have exactly one colour and the whole NRows×NRows by NCols×NCols region must be covered.
#count{R, C, Color : output(R, C, Color)} = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

% Determine number of rows and columns of the input (zero‑based)
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

output(R, C, Color) :-
    hasCopy(R, C, Color),
    not existsHigher(R, C, Color).

hasCopy(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR, SC, Color),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = #eval(SR * NRows + TR),
    C = #eval(SC * NCols + TC).

existsHigher(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR2, SC2, Value2),
    Value2 != 0,

    input(TR2, TC2, _),
    R = #eval(SR2 * NRows + TR2),
    C = #eval(SC2 * NCols + TC2).

% The whole output grid must be filled exactly once
#count{R, C, Color : output(R, C, Color)} = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.

let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

output(R, C, Color) :-
    hasCopy(R, C, Color),
    not existsHigher(R, C, Color).

hasCopy(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR, SC, Color),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = #eval(SR * NRows + TR),

existsHigher(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR2, SC2, Value2),
    Value2 != 0,
    Value2 > Color,
    input(TR2, TC2, _),

    C = #eval(SC2 * NCols + TC2).

refinements.prompt:
<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<example_1>
<example_grid_1>

<diagram>
0 7 7
7 7 7
0 7 7
</diagram>

0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7

0 7 7 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
0 0 0 7 7 7 7 7 7
0 0 0 0 7 7 0 7 7
</diagram>
</example_grid_1>

0 0 0 0 4 0 0 0 0
</diagram>
</example_grid_2>

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Input size (optional – kept for completeness)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 2.  Helper predicates for the outer edge of the grid
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% 3.  4‑connected neighbours (up, down, left, right)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% 4.  Boundary cells – any cell on the first/last row or column
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_); R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = min_col(_); R = _.

% 5.  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour (wall = 1)
outside(R,C)  :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% 6.  Default – every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% 7.  Interior colour‑1 cells (colour 1 is the “wall”) become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% 8.  Force a single answer set – the rules above determine a unique
%    interpretation of the transformation.
:- 1.
</asp>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(NRows) :- NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NCols) :- NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Tile the input.
%  * Non‑zero cells copy the whole input – they overwrite any later
%    (lower‑priority) copies.
%  * Zero cells copy the whole input but are only applied where no
%    later copy (i.e. a non‑zero copy) has already written the cell.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

%  Non‑zero source copies (high priority)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(SR,SC,V),      V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

%  Zero source copies (low priority)
output(R,C,Tile) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,Tile),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).               % no higher‑priority copy writes here

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce exactly one colour per output cell and that the whole
%  output region (NRows × NRows by NCols × NCols) is covered.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C : output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
let max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let NRows = max_row + 1.
let NCols = max_col + 1.

% copy the whole input for a non‑zero source cell
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(SR, SC, Vsrc), Vsrc != 0,
    input(TR, TC, V),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC.

% zero block for a zero source cell
output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(SR, SC, 0),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R, C, _).

% exactly one colour for every output cell and the whole output region is covered
:- #count{R, C : output(R, C, _) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

output(R, C, Color) :-
    hasCopy(R, C, Color),
    not existsHigher(R, C, Color).

hasCopy(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR, SC, Color),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = #eval(SR * NRows + TR),
    C = #eval(SC * NCols + TC).

existsHigher(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR2, SC2, Value2),
    Value2 != 0,
    Value2 > Color,
    input(TR2, TC2, _),
    R = #eval(SR2 * NRows + TR2),
    C = #eval(SC2 * NCols + TC2).

#count{R, C, Color : output(R, C, Color)} = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

% Define dimensions
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
NRows = max_row + 1.
NCols = max_col + 1.

% Determine wall color (color at top-left corner)
Wall = input(0, 0, Wall).  % Wall is integer

% Boundary definition
boundary(R, C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R, C) :- R = max_row; C = _.
boundary(R, C) :- R = _; C = 0.
boundary(R, C) :- R = _; C = max_col.

% Directions
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Flood-fill from boundary through cells that are NOT wall
reachable(R, C) :-
    boundary(R, C),
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

reachable(R2, C2) :-
    reachable(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != Wall.

% Interior: non‑wall cells that are not reachable from the boundary
interior(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall,
    not reachable(R, C).

% Output rules (exactly one per cell)
% 1. Wall cells keep the wall colour
output(R, C, Wall) :- input(R, C, Wall).

% 2. Reachable non‑wall cells become 0
output(R, C, 0) :- reachable(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

% 3. Interior zero cells become 4 (new colour for enclosed zeros)
output(R, C, 4) :- interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, 0).

% 4. Interior non‑zero cells keep their original colour
output(R, C, V) :- interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, V), V != 0.

% Ensure every cell of the grid gets exactly one colour
#count{R, C, Colour : output(R, C, Colour)} = NRows * NCols.

% No other integrity constraints

% (End of program)

% Determine dimensions
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
NRows = max_row + 1.
NCols = max_col + 1.

% Determine wall colour (colour at (0,0))
Wall = input(0,0,_).   % variable

% Boundary predicate
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = 0; C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = max_row; C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _; C = 0.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _; C = max_col.

% Directions
delta(0,1).
delta(0,-1).
delta(1,0).
delta(-1,0).

% Flood-fill outward through cells that are NOT Wall
reachable(R, C) :-
    boundary(R, C),
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

reachable(R2, C2) :-
    reachable(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != Wall.

% Interior: non-Wall cells not reachable
interior(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall,
    not reachable(R, C).

% Output rules (exactly one per cell)
% 1. Wall cells stay Wall
output(R, C, Wall) :- input(R, C, Wall).

% 2. Reachable non-Wall become 0
output(R, C, 0) :- reachable(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

% 3. Interior zero cells become 4 (new colour for enclosed zeros)
output(R, C, 4) :- interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, 0).

% 4. Interior non-zero cells keep original colour
output(R, C, V) :- interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, V), V != 0.

% Ensure each cell of the output grid is assigned exactly one colour
#count{R, C, Colour : output(R, C, Colour)} = NRows * NCols.

```asp
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the input grid
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
NRows = max_row + 1.            % number of rows  (0 .. max_row)
NCols = max_col + 1.            % number of columns (0 .. max_col)

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The colour that appears at the top‑left corner is the
%  “wall” colour – it is unchanged on the border.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
Wall = input(0, 0, Wall).       % Wall is the colour at (0,0)

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells on the outer edge of the grid
% --------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = 0;          C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = max_row;    C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _;          C = 0.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _;          C = max_col.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour.
%  All such cells reachable from the boundary are marked
%  as “reachable”.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R, C) :-
    boundary(R, C),
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

reachable(R2, C2) :-
    reachable(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are not reachable and are not the wall colour
%  are “interior”.  Inside the interior:
%    – a 0 becomes the special colour 4,
%    – any other colour stays unchanged.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall,
    not reachable(R, C).

output(R, C, Wall) :-               % wall cells stay the wall colour
    input(R, C, Wall).

output(R, C, 0) :-                  % reachable non‑wall cells become 0
    reachable(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

output(R, C, 4) :-                  % interior zero cells become 4
    interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, 0).

output(R, C, V) :-                  % interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
    interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, V), V != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every cell of the output grid must have exactly one colour.
%  The output grid has the same dimensions as the input grid.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
#count{R, C, Colour : output(R, C, Colour)} = NRows * NCols.
```

Wall = input(0, 0, Wall).       % Wall is the colour at (0,0)

boundary(R, C) :-
    R = 0;          C = _.

% --------------------------------------------------------------

delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------

% --------------------------------------------------------------

reachable(R2, C2) :-
    reachable(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are not reachable and are not the wall colour
%  are “interior”.  Inside the interior:
%    – a 0 becomes the special colour 4,
%    – any other colour stays unchanged.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall,
    not reachable(R, C).

output(R, C, Wall) :-               % wall cells stay the wall colour
    input(R, C, Wall).

output(R, C, 0) :-                  % reachable non‑wall cells become 0
    reachable(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

output(R, C, 4) :-                  % interior zero cells become 4

<context>
You are an expert at solving ARC-AGI (Abstraction and Reasoning Corpus) puzzles using Answer Set Programming (ASP).

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.
</context>

<rules>
- `input(row, col, color)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- `color(0..9)` facts are injected automatically — do not define them.
- the output predicate is always `output(row, col, color)` — do not redefine it.
- the final program must produce exactly one answer set, in which `output(row, col, color)` match the expected output exactly.
</rules>

Below are two fully worked examples.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Input size (optional – kept for completeness)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 2.  Helper predicates for the outer edge of the grid
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% 3.  4‑connected neighbours (up, down, left, right)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% 4.  Boundary cells – any cell on the first/last row or column
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_); R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = min_col(_); R = _.

% 5.  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour (wall = 1)
outside(R,C)  :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% 6.  Default – every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% 7.  Interior colour‑1 cells (colour 1 is the “wall”) become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% 8.  Force a single answer set – the rules above determine a unique
%    interpretation of the transformation.
:- 1.
</asp>

<attempt_2>
<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(NRows) :- NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NCols) :- NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Tile the input.
%  * Non‑zero cells copy the whole input – they overwrite any later
%    (lower‑priority) copies.
%  * Zero cells copy the whole input but are only applied where no
%    later copy (i.e. a non‑zero copy) has already written the cell.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

%  Non‑zero source copies (high priority)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(SR,SC,V),      V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

%  Zero source copies (low priority)
output(R,C,Tile) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,Tile),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).               % no higher‑priority copy writes here

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce exactly one colour per output cell and that the whole
%  output region (NRows × NRows by NCols × NCols) is covered.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C : output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
let max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let NRows = max_row + 1.
let NCols = max_col + 1.

% copy the whole input for a non‑zero source cell
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(SR, SC, Vsrc), Vsrc != 0,
    input(TR, TC, V),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC.

% zero block for a zero source cell
output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(SR, SC, 0),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R, C, _).

% exactly one colour for every output cell and the whole output region is covered
:- #count{R, C : output(R, C, _) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

output(R, C, Color) :-
    hasCopy(R, C, Color),
    not existsHigher(R, C, Color).

hasCopy(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR, SC, Color),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = #eval(SR * NRows + TR),
    C = #eval(SC * NCols + TC).

existsHigher(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR2, SC2, Value2),
    Value2 != 0,
    Value2 > Color,
    input(TR2, TC2, _),
    R = #eval(SR2 * NRows + TR2),
    C = #eval(SC2 * NCols + TC2).

#count{R, C, Color : output(R, C, Color)} = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the input grid
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
NRows = max_row + 1.            % number of rows  (0 .. max_row)
NCols = max_col + 1.            % number of columns (0 .. max_col)

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The colour that appears at the top‑left corner is the
%  “wall” colour – it is unchanged on the border.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
Wall = input(0, 0, Wall).       % Wall is the colour at (0,0)

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells on the outer edge of the grid
% --------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = 0;          C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = max_row;    C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _;          C = 0.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _;          C = max_col.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour.
%  All such cells reachable from the boundary are marked
%  as “reachable”.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R, C) :-
    boundary(R, C),
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

reachable(R2, C2) :-
    reachable(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are not reachable and are not the wall colour
%  are “interior”.  Inside the interior:
%    – a 0 becomes the special colour 4,
%    – any other colour stays unchanged.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall,
    not reachable(R, C).

output(R, C, Wall) :-               % wall cells stay the wall colour
    input(R, C, Wall).

output(R, C, 0) :-                  % reachable non‑wall cells become 0
    reachable(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

output(R, C, 4) :-                  % interior zero cells become 4
    interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, 0).

output(R, C, V) :-                  % interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
    interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, V), V != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every cell of the output grid must have exactly one colour.
%  The output grid has the same dimensions as the input grid.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
#count{R, C, Colour : output(R, C, Colour)} = NRows * NCols.
</asp>

Thus algorithm:

1. Determine NRows, NCols.
2. Determine max row index (Rmax), min row index (Rmin=0). Determine max col (Cmax), min col (Cmin=0).
3. Determine boundary cells: cells on any outer edge (R = 0, R = max, C = 0, C = max).
4. Determine set of colors that appear in interior (i.e., appear in some cell (R,C) that is not boundary).
5. The wall color is the (unique) color among interior that does NOT appear on any boundary cell. There should be exactly one such color; if multiple, perhaps choose one with smallest ID? But for given examples should be unique.
6. Then define reachable: cells reachable from boundary via 4-connected moves staying in cells whose color != wall (i.e., open cells). Starting points: boundary cells that are NOT wall (i.e., cells on boundary with value != wall). BFS through open cells (value != wall) to mark reachable.
7. Define output:
   - If input(R,C,Wall): output(R,C,Wall).
   - Else if reachable(R,C): output(R,C,0). (Note: reachable non-wall become 0)
   - Else (i.e., interior cells not reachable and not wall): if input(R,C,0) then output(R,C,4) else output(R,C,V) (keep same).
   - For any cell where input(R,C,V) and V != 0 and not reachable and not wall: output unchanged (i.e., V).
   - Ensure exactly one color per cell.

% dimensions
NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% boundary indices
maxRow = NRows - 1.
maxCol = NCols - 1.

% wall color (hardcoded)
Wall = 3.

% fill color (hardcoded)
Fill = 4.

% neighbor offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% boundary non-wall start cells
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (R = maxRow) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (C = 0) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (C = maxCol) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% reachable cells via 4-connectivity through non-wall cells
reachable(R,C) :-
    boundary_non_wall(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :-
    reachable(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% Output rules
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    input(R,C,Wall).

output(R,C,0) :-
    reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

output(R,C,Fill) :-
    not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,0).

output(R,C,V) :-
    not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != Fill.

% Ensure exactly one color per cell and that cells are within input dimensions
% The output region should be exactly NRows x NCols
:- #count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = NRows * NCols.

% dimensions
NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

maxRow = NRows - 1.
maxCol = NCols - 1.

% wall color
Wall = 3.
Fill = 4.

% neighbor offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% boundary non-wall start cells
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (R = 0) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (R = maxRow) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (C = 0) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary_non_wall(R,C) :-
    (C = maxCol) ; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% reachable through non-wall cells
reachable(R,C) :- boundary_non_wall(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :-
    reachable(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% Output rules
output(R,C,Wall) :-
    input(R,C,Wall).

output(R,C,0) :-
    reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

output(R,C,Fill) :-
    not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,0).

output(R,C,V) :-
    not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != Fill.

% Ensure exactly one colour per cell and correct dimensions
:- #count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = NRows * NCols.

% Compute max row and column indices
max_row = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
Wall = 3.

% neighbor offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% boundary cells that are not the wall
left_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
right_boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
top_boundary(R,C) :- C = 0; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
bottom_boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% cells reachable from the boundary without crossing a wall cell
reachable(R,C) :- left_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- right_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- top_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- bottom_boundary(R,C).

reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC, input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% Output rules
output(R,C,Wall) :- input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the grid dimensions (rows are indexed 0..max_row, cols 0..max_col)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
Wall = 3.                       % colour that forms the wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells on the outer edge that are not the wall colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
left_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;        input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
right_boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
top_boundary(R,C)   :- C = 0;        input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
bottom_boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- left_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- right_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- top_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- bottom_boundary(R,C).

reachable(R2,C2) :-
    reachable(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Output definition
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Wall) :- input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,0)   :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
output(R,C,4)   :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,V)   :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell and the output region has the same
% size as the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C,Col : output(R,C,Col)} = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V)}.
:- 1.
</asp>

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the grid dimensions (rows are indexed 0..max_row, cols 0..max_col)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
Wall = 3.                       % colour that forms the wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells on the outer edge that are not the wall colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
left_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;        input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
right_boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
top_boundary(R,C)   :- C = 0;        input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
bottom_boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- left_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- right_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- top_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- bottom_boundary(R,C).

reachable(R2,C2) :-
    reachable(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Output definition
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Wall) :- input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,0)   :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
output(R,C,4)   :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,V)   :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell and the output region has the same
% size as the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C,Col : output(R,C,Col)} = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V)}.
:- 1.

ARC-AGI puzzles show input-output grid transformations. Each cell contains a color value (integer 0–9). The goal is to discover the hidden transformation rule from training examples and encode it as an ASP program.

<transformation>
The input is treated as a tile map. The output is NxM times larger than the input (where the input is N rows x M cols). Each input cell at (BR, BC) with a non-zero value causes a copy of the full input to be placed in the corresponding NxM block of the output (at rows BR*N .. BR*N+N-1, cols BC*M .. BC*M+M-1). Each input cell with value 0 produces an all-zero block at that position.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Count distinct row/col indices — equals grid dimensions for 0-indexed inputs
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(N) :- N = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% Output is NR*NR rows and NC*NC cols (input tiled by itself)
output_cell(R, C) :-
    input(BR, BC, _), input(DR, DC, _),
    n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
    R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC.

1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Non-zero block: the sub-cell must equal the input value at the relative position
:- input(BR, BC, V), V != 0,
   input(DR, DC, TileColor),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != TileColor.

% Zero block: every sub-cell must be 0
:- input(BR, BC, 0),
   input(DR, DC, _),
   n_rows(NR), n_cols(NC),
   R = BR*NR + DR, C = BC*NC + DC,
   output(R, C, ActualColor), ActualColor != 0.
</asp>
</example_1>

<transformation>
The output is the same size as the input. Color-3 cells are unchanged. Non-3 cells (value 0) that are enclosed by color-3 walls — i.e., cannot reach the grid boundary via 4-connected movement through non-3 cells — are filled with color 4. All other non-3 cells remain 0.
</transformation>

<asp>
% Output has the same dimensions as the input
output_cell(R, C) :- input(R, C, _).
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C).

% Grid boundary coordinates
min_row(R) :- R = #min{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
max_row(R) :- R = #max{Row : input(Row, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{Col : input(_, Col, _)}.

% 4-connected adjacency offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Non-3 cells on the grid boundary are reachable from outside
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_row(Rmin), R = Rmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_row(Rmax), R = Rmax.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, min_col(Cmin), C = Cmin.
outside(R, C) :- input(R, C, V), V != 3, max_col(Cmax), C = Cmax.

% Flood-fill outward through non-3 cells
outside(R2, C2) :-
    outside(R1, C1), delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != 3.

% Color-3 cells stay 3
:- input(R, C, 3), output(R, C, Color), Color != 3.

% Outside non-3 cells stay 0
:- outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 0.

% Interior non-3 cells (enclosed by 3s, not reachable from boundary) become 4
:- input(R, C, 0), not outside(R, C), output(R, C, Color), Color != 4.
</asp>
</example_2>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 1.  Input size (optional – kept for completeness)
n_rows(N) :- N = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(M) :- M = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% 2.  Helper predicates for the outer edge of the grid
max_row(R) :- R = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col(C) :- C = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
min_row(R) :- R = #min{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
min_col(C) :- C = #min{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.

% 3.  4‑connected neighbours (up, down, left, right)
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% 4.  Boundary cells – any cell on the first/last row or column
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- R = min_row(_); C = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col(_); R = _.
boundary(R,C) :- C = min_col(_); R = _.

% 5.  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour (wall = 1)
outside(R,C)  :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 1.
outside(R2,C2) :- outside(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1+DR, C2 = C1+DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != 1.

% 6.  Default – every cell keeps its original colour
output(R,C,Col) :-
    input(R,C,Col).

% 7.  Interior colour‑1 cells (colour 1 is the “wall”) become colour 2
output(R,C,2) :-
    input(R,C,1),
    not outside(R,C).

% 8.  Force a single answer set – the rules above determine a unique
%    interpretation of the transformation.
:- 1.
</asp>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the dimensions of the input grid
n_rows(NRows) :- NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
n_cols(NCols) :- NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Tile the input.
%  * Non‑zero cells copy the whole input – they overwrite any later
%    (lower‑priority) copies.
%  * Zero cells copy the whole input but are only applied where no
%    later copy (i.e. a non‑zero copy) has already written the cell.
% --------------------------------------------------------------

%  Non‑zero source copies (high priority)
output(R,C,Color) :-
    input(SR,SC,V),      V != 0,
    input(TR,TC,_),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    Color = V.

%  Zero source copies (low priority)
output(R,C,Tile) :-
    input(SR,SC,0),
    input(TR,TC,Tile),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R,C,_).               % no higher‑priority copy writes here

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Enforce exactly one colour per output cell and that the whole
%  output region (NRows × NRows by NCols × NCols) is covered.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C : output(R,C,_) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

let max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let NRows = max_row + 1.
let NCols = max_col + 1.

% copy the whole input for a non‑zero source cell
output(R, C, V) :-
    input(SR, SC, Vsrc), Vsrc != 0,
    input(TR, TC, V),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC.

% zero block for a zero source cell
output(R, C, 0) :-
    input(SR, SC, 0),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = SR * NRows + TR,
    C = SC * NCols + TC,
    not output(R, C, _).

% exactly one colour for every output cell and the whole output region is covered
:- #count{R, C : output(R, C, _) } = NRows * NRows * NCols * NCols.
</asp>

output(R, C, Color) :-
    hasCopy(R, C, Color),
    not existsHigher(R, C, Color).

hasCopy(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR, SC, Color),
    input(TR, TC, _),
    R = #eval(SR * NRows + TR),
    C = #eval(SC * NCols + TC).

existsHigher(R, C, Color) :-
    input(SR2, SC2, Value2),

    C = #eval(SC2 * NCols + TC2).

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the input grid
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
NRows = max_row + 1.            % number of rows  (0 .. max_row)
NCols = max_col + 1.            % number of columns (0 .. max_col)

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  The colour that appears at the top‑left corner is the
%  “wall” colour – it is unchanged on the border.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
Wall = input(0, 0, Wall).       % Wall is the colour at (0,0)

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells on the outer edge of the grid
% --------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = 0;          C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = max_row;    C = _.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _;          C = 0.
boundary(R, C) :-
    R = _;          C = max_col.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbours
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0, 1).
delta(0, -1).
delta(1, 0).
delta(-1, 0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour.
%  All such cells reachable from the boundary are marked
%  as “reachable”.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R, C) :-
    boundary(R, C),
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

reachable(R2, C2) :-
    reachable(R1, C1),
    delta(DR, DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2, C2, V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells that are not reachable and are not the wall colour
%  are “interior”.  Inside the interior:
%    – a 0 becomes the special colour 4,
%    – any other colour stays unchanged.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R, C) :-
    input(R, C, V), V != Wall,
    not reachable(R, C).

output(R, C, Wall) :-               % wall cells stay the wall colour
    input(R, C, Wall).

output(R, C, 0) :-                  % reachable non‑wall cells become 0
    reachable(R, C), input(R, C, V), V != Wall.

output(R, C, 4) :-                  % interior zero cells become 4
    interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, 0).

output(R, C, V) :-                  % interior non‑zero cells keep their colour
    interior(R, C, _), input(R, C, V), V != 0.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every cell of the output grid must have exactly one colour.
%  The output grid has the same dimensions as the input grid.
% --------------------------------------------------------------
#count{R, C, Colour : output(R, C, Colour)} = NRows * NCols.
</asp>

<asp>
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Determine the grid dimensions (rows are indexed 0..max_row, cols 0..max_col)
% --------------------------------------------------------------
max_row = #max{R2 : input(R2, _, _)}.
max_col = #max{C2 : input(_, C2, _)}.
Wall = 3.                       % colour that forms the wall
% --------------------------------------------------------------
% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
% --------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Cells on the outer edge that are not the wall colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
left_boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;        input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
right_boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
top_boundary(R,C)   :- C = 0;        input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
bottom_boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
% --------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- left_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- right_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- top_boundary(R,C).
reachable(R,C) :- bottom_boundary(R,C).

reachable(R2,C2) :-
    reachable(R1,C1),
    delta(DR,DC),
    R2 = R1 + DR,
    C2 = C1 + DC,
    input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Output definition
% --------------------------------------------------------------
output(R,C,Wall) :- input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,0)   :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
output(R,C,4)   :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,0).
output(R,C,V)   :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != Wall.

% --------------------------------------------------------------
% Exactly one colour per cell and the output region has the same
% size as the input
% --------------------------------------------------------------
:- #count{R,C,Col : output(R,C,Col)} = #count{R,C,V : input(R,C,V)}.
:- 1.
</asp>
