% Determine dimensions
let max_row = #max{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let max_col = #max{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let NRows = max_row + 1.
let NCols = max_col + 1.

% Determine wall colour (value at (0,0))
wall(Wall) :- input(0, 0, Wall).

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells that are not the wall colour (these start the flood fill)
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC, input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% Interior cells: not reachable and not on boundary
interior(R,C) :- not reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% Count interior non‑wall colours to pick the one with smallest (row,col) order
interior_nonwall(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall, V != 0.

% Choose the first interior non‑wall cell in row‑major order as the interior colour
intColour(V) :-
    interior_nonwall(R,C,V),
    not exists_other_int(R,C,V).

exists_other_int(R,C,V) :-
    interior_nonwall(R1,C1,V1),
    V1 != Wall,
    V1 != 0,
    R1 < R;
    or (R1 = R, C1 < C).

% Output definitions
output(R,C,Wall) :- input(R,C,Wall).

output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

output(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != 0, V != Wall, not interior_wall(R,C).   % interior_wall will be handled separately

output(R,C,Int) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,Wall), intColour(Int), Int != Wall.

output(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).   % interior zero becomes 4

% For completeness, also handle cells that are boundary but not wall (they are reachable) already covered

% Ensure each cell gets exactly one colour and whole output region is covered
#count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = (max_row + 1) * (max_col + 1).
:- 1.

% Determine dimensions of the grid (rows and columns are indexed from 0)
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let max_row = NRows - 1.
let max_col = NCols - 1.

% Wall colour – colour at (0,0)
wall(Wall) :- input(0, 0, Wall).

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Cells on the outer edge that are NOT the wall colour
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;       C = _; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row;  C = _; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = _;       C = 0;  input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = _;       C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% Interior cells: not reachable and not on the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not reachable(R,C), not boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% Interior cells that are NOT the wall and NOT zero – these are the
% candidates for the colour that will be copied into interior walls.
interior_nonwall(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                        V != Wall, V != 0.

% The colour of the first interior non‑wall cell (row‑major order)
intColour(Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R,C,Colour),
    not exists_earlier(R,C,Colour).

exists_earlier(R,C,Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R1,C1,V1),
    V1 != Wall,
    V1 != 0,
    (R1 < R);
    or (R1 = R, C1 < C).

% Cells that are walls on the boundary stay wall
output(R,C,Wall) :- input(R,C,Wall).

% Reachable non‑wall cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% Interior zero cells become 4 (as in the reference example)
output(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).

% Interior non‑zero cells keep their own colour
output(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                V != 0, V != Wall,
                not interior_wall(R,C).

% Interior walls copy the colour found for interior walls
interior_wall(R,C) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,Colour) :- interior_wall(R,C), intColour(Colour), Colour != Wall.

% Every cell of the output grid must have exactly one colour
#count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = (max_row + 1) * (max_col + 1).
:- 1.
</asp>

<asp>
% Determine grid dimensions
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let max_row = NRows - 1.
let max_col = NCols - 1.

% Wall colour (colour at (0,0))
wall(Wall) :- input(0, 0, Wall).

% 4‑connected neighbour offsets
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% Boundary cells that are NOT the wall colour
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;        C = _; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row;    C = _; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = _;        C = 0;  input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = _;        C = max_col; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC), R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% Interior cells: not reachable and not on the boundary
interior(R,C) :- not reachable(R,C), not boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% Interior cells that are not wall and not zero – candidates for the interior colour
interior_nonwall(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                         V != Wall, V != 0.

% The colour of the first interior non‑wall cell (row‑major order)
intColour(Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R,C,Colour),
    not exists_earlier(R,C,Colour).

exists_earlier(R,C,Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R1,C1,V1),
    V1 != Wall,
    V1 != 0,
    (R1 < R);
    or (R1 = R, C1 < C).

% Output rules
% Boundary walls keep the wall colour
output(R,C,Wall) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).

% Reachable non‑wall cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% Interior zero cells become 4 (as in the reference example)
output(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).

% Interior non‑zero, non‑wall cells keep their own colour
output(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                V != 0, V != Wall,
                not interior_wall(R,C).

% Interior walls copy the colour chosen for interior walls
interior_wall(R,C) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,Colour) :- interior_wall(R,C), intColour(Colour), Colour != Wall.

% Exactly one colour per output cell and all cells are covered
#count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = (max_row + 1) * (max_col + 1).
:- 1.
</asp>

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the input grid (rows and columns are 0‑based)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let max_row = NRows - 1.
let max_col = NCols - 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall colour – colour at the top‑left corner (0,0)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
wall(Wall) :- input(0, 0, Wall).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells on the outer edge that are NOT the wall colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col;  input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                   R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                   input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – not reachable and not on the boundary
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- not reachable(R,C), not boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells that are NOT the wall and NOT zero.
%  These are the candidates for the colour that will be copied into
%  interior walls.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior_nonwall(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                          V != Wall, V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  The colour of the first interior non‑wall cell (row‑major order)
%  – this colour will be used for all interior walls.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
intColour(Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R,C,Colour),
    not exists_earlier(R,C,Colour).

exists_earlier(R,C,Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R1,C1,V1),
    V1 != Wall,
    V1 != 0,
    (R1 < R);
    or (R1 = R, C1 < C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definitions
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Boundary walls stay wall colour

%  2.  Reachable non‑wall cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

%  3.  Interior zero cells become 4 (as in the reference example)
output(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).

%  4.  Interior non‑zero, non‑wall cells keep their own colour
output(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                V != 0, V != Wall,
                not interior_wall(R,C).

%  5.  Interior walls copy the colour chosen for interior walls
interior_wall(R,C) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,Colour) :- interior_wall(R,C), intColour(Colour), Colour != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every output cell must have exactly one colour and the whole
%  (NRows × NCols) region must be covered.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
#count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = (max_row + 1) * (max_col + 1).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Determine the size of the input grid (rows and columns are 0‑based)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
let NRows = #count{R : input(R, _, _)}.
let NCols = #count{C : input(_, C, _)}.
let max_row = NRows - 1.
let max_col = NCols - 1.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Wall colour – colour at the top‑left corner (0,0)
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
wall(Wall) :- input(0, 0, Wall).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  4‑connected neighbourhood offsets
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
delta(0,1). delta(0,-1). delta(1,0). delta(-1,0).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Cells on the outer edge that are NOT the wall colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
boundary(R,C) :- R = 0;      input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- R = max_row; input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = 0;      input(R,C,V), V != Wall.
boundary(R,C) :- C = max_col;  input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Flood‑fill through cells that are NOT the wall colour
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
reachable(R,C) :- boundary(R,C).
reachable(R2,C2) :- reachable(R1,C1), delta(DR,DC),
                   R2 = R1 + DR, C2 = C1 + DC,
                   input(R2,C2,V), V != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells – not reachable and not on the boundary
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior(R,C) :- not reachable(R,C), not boundary(R,C), input(R,C,V).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Interior cells that are NOT the wall and NOT zero.
%  These are the candidates for the colour that will be copied into
%  interior walls.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
interior_nonwall(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                          V != Wall, V != 0.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  The colour of the first interior non‑wall cell (row‑major order)
%  – this colour will be used for all interior walls.
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
intColour(Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R,C,Colour),
    not exists_earlier(R,C,Colour).

exists_earlier(R,C,Colour) :-
    interior_nonwall(R1,C1,V1),
    V1 != Wall,
    V1 != 0,
    (R1 < R);
    or (R1 = R, C1 < C).

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Output definitions
% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  1.  Boundary walls stay wall colour
output(R,C,Wall) :- boundary(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).

%  2.  Reachable non‑wall cells become 0
output(R,C,0) :- reachable(R,C), input(R,C,V), V != Wall.

%  3.  Interior zero cells become 4 (as in the reference example)
output(R,C,4) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,0).

%  4.  Interior non‑zero, non‑wall cells keep their own colour
output(R,C,V) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,V),
                V != 0, V != Wall,
                not interior_wall(R,C).

%  5.  Interior walls copy the colour chosen for interior walls
interior_wall(R,C) :- interior(R,C), input(R,C,Wall).
output(R,C,Colour) :- interior_wall(R,C), intColour(Colour), Colour != Wall.

% ------------------------------------------------------------------
%  Every output cell must have exactly one colour and the whole
%  (NRows × NCols) region must be covered.

#count{R, C, Col : output(R, C, Col)} = (max_row + 1) * (max_col + 1).
